parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf00914-request`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.query }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Frame the Assignment · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Unpack Sections · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Review Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below — downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to Notion

### Persist Deliverable to Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per request (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Request Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Gmail · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Run an AI agent task with Gmail at scale

**Pattern:** AI Agent · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Run an AI agent task with Gmail at scale. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual research assistant is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Frame the Assignment → Coordinator Agent → Unpack Sections → Persist Deliverable to Notion → Fingerprint for Idempotency → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai agent without manual effort
- Anyone automating research assistant in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Anthropic API key; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The Coordinator Agent never does the specialist work itself — each specialist is delegated to its own sub-workflow via a Workflow Tool, so every specialist stays single-responsibility and testable in isolation instead of one giant prompt trying to do everything. An output parser forces the final answer into JSON so downstream nodes can rely on the shape instead of parsing free text.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Add a third specialist tool and update the system message to explain when the agent should reach for it.
2. Loosen or tighten the JSON schema on the output parser and see how the agent's final answer shape changes.
3. Replace one placeholder Workflow Tool with a real n8n sub-workflow and measure the round-trip latency.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your research assistant process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.

json.query: What's our refund policy for orders over $500?
